name: Social Media Management System v1
goal: Prepare the first reusable AG OS client-system package for social media content operations in draft and staging mode only.
knownFacts: This is an active product project in managed staging, not AG OS core.
The owner accepted the complete H2 staged product and operating package on 2026-07-09.
Build, target-repository merge, dedicated Netlify staging, content review, draft-content approval, and manual posting pack evidence are source controlled.

The accepted H2 package remains draft and staging only; acceptance does not authorize OAuth, account connection, posting, scheduling, analytics, messages/comments, n8n activation, paid tools, production deployment, credentials, domain/DNS, or production/customer data.
The system must support multiple clients, multiple brands per client, and multiple platform accounts per client without hardcoded client records.
Every platform account starts not connected, draft only, approval required, and live posting blocked.
No social account connection, credential, live posting, scheduling, direct message, comment, analytics API, external platform API call, production data, or customer data is authorized by this record.
scope: Maintain production-clean AG OS project metadata for Social Media Management System v1.
Operate the accepted product in managed-staging and draft-only mode.
Define client, brand, platform account, content, post package, approval, report, and activity-log models.
Define safety gates for draft-only operation and blocked live actions.
Document and validate future connector paths for GitHub, Netlify, n8n, social OAuth, analytics, scheduling, and client portal integration without activating them.
Preserve H2 acceptance, quality, staging, lesson-candidate, and runbook evidence as the baseline for future work.
outOfScope: Do not create or mutate a repository, branch, file set, or pull request outside a separately approved scope.
Do not perform another staging deploy or any production deploy without separate owner approval.
Do not use or store credentials.
Do not post, schedule, send messages, manage comments, or perform live outreach.
Do not call social, analytics, scheduling, or external platform APIs.
Do not use production or customer data.
Do not modify Lead Gen production, the AI Receptionist repository, or the Constitution.
stack: Static or lightweight app frontend when separately approved
Netlify staging only when separately approved
Source-controlled placeholder data only for structure
n8n draft workflow only when separately approved

risks.risk: Social media systems can accidentally cross into live posting, scheduling, account connection, analytics API, or credential handling.
risks.mitigation: Keep v1 in draft-only mode with live posting blocked, approval required, and every connector status not connected or blocked until separate owner approval.

risks.risk: Client-system reuse can drift into hardcoded client assumptions.
risks.mitigation: Use neutral client, brand, and account models in active records, keep unknown client values in docs/templates only, and add real clients later through owner-approved records.

risks.risk: Content operations can create unapproved costs through AI, media processing, storage, scheduling, or platform tools.
risks.mitigation: Use Bootstrap Mode, local/source-controlled structure first, and require Cost OS approval before any paid tool or live API usage.

approvalRequiredFor: file creation or update in a product repository
qualityGates: Data model supports Client, Brand, PlatformAccount, ContentItem, PostPackage, PostVariant, Approval, WeeklyReport, and ActivityLog.
Every account defaults to not connected, draft only, approval required, and live posting blocked.
No live connector, credential, paid tool, production data, or customer data is introduced.
Future build plan preserves the social media content operations archetype quality checklist.
Validation, boot check, tests, and git diff check pass before package PR merge.
Full H2 owner acceptance is recorded without granting live-operation permission.
securityReview: Production-clean metadata and docs only. No social credentials, account tokens, platform secrets, client private data, production data, live endpoints, or external API payloads are recorded.
costTracking: No paid actions authorized. Future build, connector, AI, media, storage, scheduling, analytics, or platform costs require Cost OS review and owner approval.
deploymentPlan: Dedicated Netlify staging proof exists for the accepted H2 product. Every future staging redeploy or production deployment requires a separate approval package, active approval lock, validation, boot check, target repo review, rollback evidence, and recorded connector result.
